pages.raw_text:
Within the next few days the
Great Northern Railroad Co. will
begin settlement of damages in.
Cured in the Milan wreck. More
than 25 people were injured more
or less seriously and 1t is estimated
by local attorneys who already are
beginning to prepare lawsuits, that
it will cost the company thousands
of dollars.


Division Counsel Murray is
awaiting word from st. Paul offic.
ials before taking up claims. This
Is expected soon. 1t is customary
for officials to give their attorneys
the privilege Of using certain
amount of money in making hasty
settlements and getting the com
pany released.


OF the Is injured taken to the
hospital the day of the wreck 8
were released yesterday, their in
juries not being serious enough to
confine them. Reports from the
hospital today are that the others
are doing as we'll as could be ex
pected, but none will be released
for a few days.


Conductor Charles Hollings
worth. the worst hurt, is fast i'm
proving. He Is now able to see
clearly. When seen yesterday his
eyes were badly injured and almost
closed. His broken shoulder has
been attended to and is on the
mend.


Yesterdays investigation of the
wreck by State Commissioners
Fairchild and Jones amounted to a
pleasant little trip to Milan at the
cOmpany's expense with company
officials and attorneys along. H. e
Owen and w. A. Barr, who were In
the wreck, represented the public
They both testified to the bad con
dition Of the track and were brow
